page: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance belonging to the t2a server family. It is powered by an ARMv8-based Ampere Altra processor utilizing the Neoverse-N1 architecture. The instance features 2 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.0 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage or GPU acceleration bundled with this instance.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the t2a-standard-2 is suited for lightweight workloads. In single-core stress-ng tests, the CPU achieves average performance, and single-threaded gzip compression also registers in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is weak, as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory bandwidth is relatively constrained, with large-block read and write speeds falling into the weak category, and small-block cached reads registering as poor. Cryptographic performance is limited, with the Passmark encryption test scoring in the bottom tier, although hardware-accelerated AES is supported.

The instance lacks advanced vector and matrix extensions, such as AVX or AMX, and does not support hyper-threading. This makes it less suitable for heavy mathematical modeling, ray tracing, or large-scale machine learning, as reflected in its poor Geekbench ray tracing and image processing scores. However, with a static web serving throughput of 2.66 GB/sec and 2.61 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks, it is a viable, cost-efficient option for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and general-purpose application hosting where ARM compatibility is present.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is an ARM-based general-purpose instance powered by a 3.0 GHz Ampere Altra Neoverse-N1 processor. It features 2 dedicated physical cores, 8.0 GB of system memory, and no local storage or GPU acceleration. Benchmarks show average single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, though multi-core performance and memory bandwidth are generally weak. Lacking advanced vector extensions like AVX or AMX, this instance is not designed for intensive mathematical computations or machine learning. However, with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, it provides a cost-efficient platform for lightweight web serving, basic database operations, and general-purpose ARM-native development workloads.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is an ARM-based general-purpose instance with 2 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 8 GB RAM, offering a cost-efficient option for lightweight web serving and basic database tasks.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2a-standard-2 is an ARM-based instance featuring 2 dedicated Neoverse-N1 cores and 8 GB RAM for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A cost-efficient ARM-based instance with dedicated physical cores, designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads and basic web serving.

Features 2 dedicated physical cores and 8.0 GB of system memory
Average single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
No local storage or GPU acceleration included
Best suited for lightweight web serving, basic database tasks, and ARM development
